concept.title: Required and Suggested validation levels
concept.shortdesc: Every rule in a rule set has a level: Required or Suggested. Required failures block your CI pipeline. Suggested failures appear in the report as recommendations and do not block a merge.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Understand the difference between Required and Suggested validation rules, and how each level affects your CI/CD pipeline exit code.

row.entry: When your team sets this level
CLI exits with code 1. CI pipeline fails and blocks the merge.
The check enforces a hard editorial standard — for example, every tutorial must have an ms.topic: tutorial value and a single H1.
CLI exits with code 0 even if the rule fails. The failure appears in the report but does not block the merge.
The check is a best-practice recommendation — for example, title length should be under 60 characters. Violations are noted but not enforced.

section.title: What you see
section.codeblock:
✗ [4] description-must-exist [Required] → Add a description field to the front matter.
✗ [6] title-under-60-chars [Suggested] → Title must be under 60 characters.
section.p: Rule 4 blocks your pull request. Rule 6 does not. If no level is shown, the rule defaults to Required.
